diagnoses.reasoning: Patient has markedly elevated LDL (5.6 vs <1.4 for very-high-risk), elevated total cholesterol (7.1 vs <5.2), elevated triglycerides (2.8 vs <1.7), and low HDL (0.8 vs >1.0 for males). This pattern of multiple lipid abnormalities maps to mixed dyslipidemia (E78.5).

lab_values.reasoning: LDL_Chol_mmol directly maps to LDL Cholesterol. Value of 5.6 mmol/L is drastically above the very-high-risk threshold of <1.4 mmol/L, indicating severely elevated LDL.
Total_Chol_mmol maps directly to Total Cholesterol. Value of 7.1 mmol/L exceeds the reference limit of <5.2 mmol/L.
TG_mmol is a standard abbreviation for triglycerides. Value of 2.8 mmol/L exceeds the reference limit of <1.7 mmol/L.
HDL_mmol maps to HDL Cholesterol. Value of 0.8 mmol/L is below the male reference threshold of >1.0 mmol/L, indicating low HDL.
FBG_mmol is a common abbreviation for Fasting Blood Glucose. Value of 5.2 mmol/L is within the normal reference range of 3.9-5.5 mmol/L.
HbA1c_% maps directly to HbA1c (glycated hemoglobin). Value of 5.4% is within the normal reference range of <5.7%.

Lp_a_nmol_L maps to Lipoprotein(a) [Lp(a)], a well-established cardiovascular risk biomarker. Value of 210 nmol/L is nearly 3x the upper reference limit of <75 nmol/L. No exact match in the provided OMOP reference table; LOINC 43583-4 is the standard code for Lp(a) in nmol/L.

hs_CRP_mg_L maps to high-sensitivity C-Reactive Protein (hs-CRP), an inflammatory biomarker used in cardiovascular risk stratification. Value of 4.8 mg/L is well above the <1.0 mg/L low-risk threshold. No exact match in the provided OMOP reference table; LOINC 30522-7 is the standard code for hs-CRP.

flags: Severely elevated LDL cholesterol (5.6 mmol/L vs <1.4 very-high-risk target) — urgent lipid-lowering therapy evaluation needed
Markedly elevated Lipoprotein(a) at 210 nmol/L (>75 nmol/L) — genetic/familial hyperlipidemia workup recommended
Elevated hs-CRP (4.8 mg/L) indicating high residual inflammatory cardiovascular risk
Low HDL cholesterol (0.8 mmol/L) compounding overall atherogenic lipid profile
Elevated triglycerides (2.8 mmol/L) contributing to mixed dyslipidemia
